zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes of Halton於大約1110年出生在英國越可爾韋斯特 哈爾頓時，她的父親William fitzNigel the Constable32歲，母親Adeliza27歲。她和Eustace fitz John至少有2個兒子。她去世於1166年在英國諾圖波藍德阿恩維克 卡素享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Halton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): habitational name from any of several places called Halton in Cheshire, Lancashire, Yorkshire, Northumberland, Lincolnshire, Shropshire and Buckinghamshire. Halton is usually from Old English h(e)alh ‘nook, hollow’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. Halton in Cheshire, however, is possibly named from an Old English hāthel ‘heathery place’ + tūn, and Halton in Northumberland from an Old English hāw ‘look out’ + hyll ‘hill’ + tūn.
History: Most English bearers of this name trace their descent from William de Halton, who was living at Halton, Lancashire, in 1346.

variants.name: Holton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Holton, such as those in Oxfordshire, Suffolk, Dorset, and Somerset. The Oxfordshire and Somerset placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, while the Suffolk placename comes from the Old English personal name Hōla + tūn. The Dorset placename derives from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ or holt ‘wood, grove, thicket’ + tūn.

Irish: altered form of O'Haltahan, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hUltacháin ‘descendant of Ultachán’, a diminutive of Ultach ‘Ulsterman’. This is a rare Fermanagh surname, sometimes Anglicized as Nolan .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes of Halton was born about 1110, in West Halton, Yorkshire, England, her father, William fitzNigel the Constable, was 32 and her mother, Adeliza, was 27. She had at least 2 sons with Eustace fitz John. She died in 1166, in Alnwick Castle, Northumberland, England, at the age of 57.

fr.Ambushed at Consyllt, near Basingwerk Ab: Abbey, Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes of Halton est née environ 1110, à West Halton, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William fitzNigel the Constable, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Adeliza, avait 27 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Eustace fitz John. Elle est décédée en 1166, à Alnwick Castle, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes of Halton um 1110 in West Halton, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William fitzNigel the Constable 32 und ihre Mutter Adeliza 27. Sie hatte mit Eustace fitz John mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1166 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Alnwick Castle, Northumberland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes of Halton è nata il circa 1110 nel West Halton, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, William fitzNigel the Constable, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Adeliza, aveva 27 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Eustace fitz John. Morì nel 1166, nel Alnwick Castle, Northumberland, England, all'età di 57 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes of Halton(이)가 대략 1110년 영국 요르크실르 웨스트 할턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William fitzNigel the Constable(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adeliza(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 Eustace fitz John(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1166년 영국 노르스벌랜드 알윅 카스틀에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes of Halton родилась около 1110 г. в местности вест хэлтон, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William fitzNigel the Constable было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Adeliza, 27. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Eustace fitz John. Она умерла в 1166 г. в местности алнвикк кастл, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes of Halton nació aproximadamente 1110, en West Halton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William fitzNigel the Constable, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Adeliza, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Eustace fitz John. Murió en 1166, en Alnwick Castle, Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 57 años.
